subject: Keybox integrated into sandbox/ per operator utterance 16:15Z 'use this keybox and check pi'
summary: Added sandbox/keybox/ + 2 test files. The operator-supplied keybox at C:\Users\Zonia\Desktop\keybox_20260523.xml is parsed (real Samsung-rooted device, ECDSA primary + RSA secondary, 3-cert chain per key, sha 58243fe6...) and registered in sandbox/keybox/manifest.json. The raw keybox.xml is NOT committed — only the manifest entry (sha + metadata, no key material). Operator utterance 2026-05-24T16:15:42Z resolved + acked via ack-operator-utterance.ps1.

files_new: sandbox/keybox/manifest.json (1 row registered: Samsung_c5faa186 keybox, status=active)
sandbox/tests/test_keybox.py (7 cases — 4 manifest invariants + 3 against operator keybox)
sandbox/tests/test_play_integrity.py (7 cases — 2 layer-1 invariants + 5 layer-2/3 gated)

verification.private_key_leak_check: test_manifest_no_private_key_material PASS — no PEM markers in manifest.json or any committed file in sandbox/keybox/

play_integrity_state_machine: 3-layer test surface staged. Layer 1 (no cvd, no keybox) PASSES today on Windows. Layer 2 (cvd, no keybox) skips until operator boots cvd. Layer 3 (cvd + keybox + PIFork Magisk) skips until P3 operator action (PIFork install + keybox volume-mount + PI testbed APK). When all 3 land, full PI handshake verifies the keybox produces a MEETS_DEVICE_INTEGRITY verdict from Google.
operator_action_remaining_for_PI: Linux+KVM host with cvd booted (same prereq as the original sandbox scaffold)
PIFork Magisk module installed in cvd image (operator builds from github.com/chiteroman/PlayIntegrityFork)
PI testbed APK (e.g., PlayIntegrityChecker by chiteroman) installed on cvd for the test_pi_verdict_meets_device_integrity assertion
anti-brick_unchanged: Keybox integration touches NONE of the 5 anti-brick gates — keybox is consumed only during runtime PI handshake inside cvd, never touches kernel build or boot path. Physical Pixel 6a still never touched by anything in sandbox.
